identity_gate.notes: The ETManufacturing Reuters republication identifies the Tata Electronics iPhone components factory in Hosur, Tamil Nadu, the May 25 warning notice, and the same contamination complaint cluster referenced by the queue headline. The location and date anchors are sufficient for a MATCH.
supporting_quotes: An Indian state health authority is investigating how liquid discharged from Apple supplier Tata's iPhone components factory has affected farmers, some of whom complained about skin issues from contamination in their farmlands, according to three officials and a document reviewed by Reuters.
The Tata Electronics plant in Tamil Nadu's Hosur was sent a warning notice by the state pollution control board on May 25 for allegedly contaminating groundwater in adjacent farms.

Tata said in a statement this week that the pollution board had dropped its scrutiny after confirming its analysis of recently collected water samples from inside the facility did "not indicate any contamination".

field_updates.title: Tata iPhone Parts Factory Health Probe
field_updates.accused_gap: No accused, arrests, or prosecution details were identified in the frozen source set.
field_updates.summary: Reuters, via ETManufacturing, reported that an Indian state health authority is investigating complaints from farmers near Tata Electronics' iPhone components factory in Hosur, Tamil Nadu, after a May 25 warning notice from the state pollution control board over alleged groundwater contamination in adjacent farms. The report said some farmers complained of skin issues, two water samples from the farms were sent for testing, and Tata said its analysis of recently collected water samples did not indicate contamination.

timeline.event: The state pollution control board sent Tata Electronics' Hosur plant a warning notice for allegedly contaminating groundwater in adjacent farms.

Reuters reported that an Indian state health authority was investigating how liquid discharged from Tata's iPhone components factory had affected farmers, some of whom complained about skin issues from contamination in their farmlands; the report also said two water samples from the farms were sent for testing and Tata said its analysis did not indicate contamination.

Published On Jun 19, 2026 at 02:03 PM IST
The Tata Electronics plant in Tamil Nadu's Hosur was sent a warning notice by the state pollution control board on May 25 for allegedly contaminating groundwater in adjacent farms.

An Indian state health authority is investigating how liquid discharged from Apple supplier Tata's iPhone components factory has affected farmers, some of whom complained about skin issues from contamination in their farmlands

Tata said in a statement this week that the pollution board had dropped its scrutiny after confirming its analysis of recently collected water samples from inside the facility did "not indicate any contamination"
